creator: feenberg@nber.org
text:
On Fri, 25 Sep 2009, Naram Qashat wrote:

>
> I just manually downloaded w32api-3.13-mingw32-dev.tar.gz from there, and did 
> "md5 w32api-3.13-mingw32-dev.tar.gz" and it has the correct checksum.  Can 
> you show what it said when you did make checksum?  Also, the files are 
> usually stored in /usr/ports/distfiles/mingw, so you could manually run md5 
> on the files in there and see if they match what distinfo says.

Thank you - I didn't know to look in /usr/ports/distfiles.

>
> I also just did a portsnap inside of a VM running FreeBSD 8.0-RC1 from the 
> same server you did (portsnap2.freebsd.org), deleted anything in 
> /usr/ports/distfiles/mingw, and then I did the following from a csh shell: 
> env 
> MASTER_SITE_SOURCEFORGE=http://freefr.dl.sourceforge.net/project/%SUBDIR%/ 
> make checksum.  Says checksum OK for all 3 files.  I'm really not sure what 
> is going on with your end, but I am not seeing any of the problems you are.
>

Here is the "typescript" from preforming that experiment. I printed 
the actual checksums (and distinfo) at the end.

Can you compare the checksum I get (a50fff...) with yours? Am I getting 
the same file? Notice I did remove the old files before running make. I 
would add that if I don't specify the server, the makefile tries a large 
number of other servers for the w32api file, and that is the only file 
that is not on the first server tried.

I have added blank lines just before the interesting lines:

========================================
Script started, output file is typescript
You have mail.

freebsd72# /bin/rm -f /usr/ports/distfiles/mingw/*
freebsd72# env MASTER_SITE_SOURCEFORGE=http://freefr.dl.sourceforge.net/project/%SUBDIR%/ make checksum
